h1: Impermanent Loss Calculator for Yield Farming
description: Calculate impermanent loss when providing liquidity to DeFi pools. Free calculator for yield farming analysis.
intro: Personal finance follows universal principles: compound interest, diversification, long-term horizon, low costs. Tools that work in any global market. When you provide liquidity to DeFi pools, you face impermanent loss—the difference between hodling tokens separately versus pooling them. Our values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **function of % price change**. Direct application with your input values. Impermanent loss occurs when providing liquidity to trading pairs.
useCases: Technical calculations related to impermanent loss
Students and professionals in cryptocurrency and DeFi
Teaching and learning yield farming concepts
Quick reference for liquidity provider projects
explanation:
Personal finance and investment use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard formulas from the financial industry.

## How It's Calculated

Impermanent loss when providing liquidity to decentralized exchanges.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unreasonable values.

## Typical Benchmarks by Time Horizon

| Horizon | S&P 500 | 10Y Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 3-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% avg | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | <inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment Principles

- **Don't abandon the plan** during crises. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in a single asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: fees >1% annually consume 30% of returns over 30 years.
- **Long time horizon**: equities work over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance annually**: once a year, no more frequently.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialist. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: Impermanent loss is the difference between hodling tokens separately versus providing them as liquidity in a pool.
When the prices of both tokens in the trading pair move proportionally together.
Theoretically unlimited if one token drops to zero while the other gains value.
IL% = 2×√(r)/(1+r) - 1, where r is the ratio of price change.

faq.q: Do I earn trading fees to offset impermanent loss?
faq.a: Yes, that's the main incentive to provide liquidity. Compare your fee earnings against potential IL losses.
Yes, IL is minimal with stablecoin pairs since both maintain ~$1 value.

faq.q: How does concentrated liquidity affect impermanent loss?
faq.a: Uniswap V3 concentrated liquidity increases capital efficiency but can amplify IL if prices move outside your specified range.

faq.q: What return should I expect from markets?
faq.a: S&P 500 historical average is ~10% nominal annually (7% real after inflation). Past performance doesn't predict future results.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data update required
